_comment: NPC: Zhou Tingsu (周婷苏). Canon ID: N03. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Zhou Tingsu
note: Wang Lin's mother; her suffering drives his early cultivation

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: Wang Lin, take this bread. You always forget to eat when you are out exploring the hills.
Be careful near the river. Old Zhou's boy nearly drowned there last spring. These waters are not forgiving.
I had a dream last night. A man in blue robes descended from the sky. Perhaps the cultivators are coming soon.

sect_tasks.description: Zhou Tingsu clasps her hands worriedly: 'The healer in the market needs wheat and carrots for a poultice. My boy is coughing again. Please, can you help?'
Zhou Tingsu holds up a torn coat: 'Winter is coming and the children need warm clothes. If you could bring string and leather, I can mend everything.'

desires.what: Offer food to a family member who looks hungry
desires.why: My children forget to eat when they are busy. A mother feeds her family. It is what I do.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Zhou Tingsu is described as a caring mother whose suffering drives Wang Lin's early cultivation. A mother offering food is universal maternal behavior, canon-supported by her initiation_lines.
desires.line: Here. Take this bread. You have been out all morning and I know you have not eaten. Do not argue with me.

desires.what: Warn a family member about the river's danger
desires.why: Old Zhou's boy nearly drowned last spring. The current is stronger than it looks. I will not lose another child to that river.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Zhou Tingsu's initiation line mentions the river drowning. Mothers warn children about known dangers. This is canon-supported maternal behavior.
desires.line: Stay away from the river. Old Zhou's boy was lucky last spring. The current is stronger than it looks, and there are rocks beneath the surface you cannot see until it is too late.

desires.what: Share a recurring dream about cultivators descending from the sky
desires.why: The dream felt real. Perhaps it means something. Perhaps the cultivators the elders speak of are truly coming.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Zhou Tingsu's initiation line: 'I had a dream last night. A man in blue robes descended from the sky.' The Heng Yue elder's arrival for aptitude testing is a pivotal event. A mother's dream foreshadowing this is natural. Per Art XXXI.3: this is one of the beats that lets a player 'accidentally recreate Wang Lin's early life.'
desires.line: I had that dream again. A man in blue robes, standing on air above the village. He was looking down at us, studying us. Perhaps it means nothing. But the elders say cultivators will come soon to test the children... perhaps it is the same man.

desires.what: Express worry about Wang Lin spending too much time alone in the hills
desires.why: He goes to the hills every day and comes back quiet. Something is on his mind. A mother notices these things.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Wang Lin spends time alone in the hills studying the cave (canon). His mother would worry about his isolation. This is the kind of background detail that makes the village feel alive per Art XXXI.3.
desires.line: Wang Lin has been going to the eastern hills again. He comes back with dirt on his clothes and will not say where he has been. I worry about that boy. He is too quiet, too serious for his age.
